item.title: I still can't believe AOC has my oil painting. It was my first portrait with oil paints too. I did it when I was 16. She hung it in her office inside the Capitol. It's such an honor. ?
item.dc:creator: AnisJerbi

item.title: Forgot About Him And That Sucks
item.description: This awesome individual, Riley Howell, knocked down an active shooter in his anthropology class at University North Carolina in 2019. Although he was killed in the process, along with another student, he possibly saved more lives. He was in ROTC and wished to join the military after college. I had forgotten about him this past year. I didn't know him. He was just a news blip that faded quickly as more world chaos took his place. Something about this picture made me want to always remember him. To remember his act and sacrifice and always honor that. I looked him up and found out he was buried with full military honors and was awarded the Civilian Medal Of Honor, the highest medal awarded to non military. I'm sorry I forgot about you for awhile Riley. Toast you tonight with a little mezcal and sincere thank you.
item.dc:creator: ChicanoBatman

item.title: This is done to me, I've done it to other people. I needed to hear this, maybe somone else can learn from it too.
item.description: My partner has some comprehension issues, and I have done this a lot. I feel like a monster. Similarly, I DO NOT like crowds. There are few things as akward to me as having a room full of people simultaneously tur and shout "HEY! YOU DECIDED TO SHOW UP!!!" I know to "normal" people it feels welcoming, but people like me, it is hard to not turn an run.
item.dc:creator: dickblack12

item.title: Made It Another Year
item.description: “I’ll try anything once” is what I drunkenly said before hitting the pipe for the first time and opening one of the darkest chapters of my life. After coming down for the first time I felt like the biggest fucking idiot and was so pissed at myself. I couldn’t believe that I had actually tried meth and I was scared of how much I had enjoyed it. I told myself that quitting right then and there would probably be my easiest shot at putting it behind me otherwise my life was over. I made it about a week before I caved to the temptation and then I was absolutely hooked. The several days long benders began. The changes to my appearance happened scary fast and I was deeply disturbed and saddened by it but I just couldn’t stop. It was always “just let me get through this work week and I’m done” but weeks turned into months. My problem was made exponentially worse when I lost a member of my family to especially devastating circumstances and the trauma mixed with addiction was the hellish combo that drove me into the deepest blackest mental hole I’ve ever been in. I hit a new rock bottom I never imagined I could reach. I felt and looked like my soul and every spark of life had been stripped from me and I would lock myself away in my room for days at a time tweaked out and whispering to myself over and over that I was ready for death. I felt like I was 3/4 dead and looked like an anorexic corpse. But long story short it was my desire to be there for my family and not add to the already huge trauma my family was going trough that ultimately helped to snap me out of it. The day my supplier said he ran out and wasn’t sure when he could get his hands on more, I told myself this was my best shot at breaking away and I ran. I quit my job, ghosted the bad influences in my life and locked myself away in my room stocked with food. When I wasn’t sleeping, I was stuffing my face and watching a continuous stream of YouTube videos until would pass out again. Rinse and repeat. It helped to distract me and get me cleaned up and now here I am 3 years later sober and working a stable and better paying job. It has taken a long time for me to start feeling “normal” again because that shit can really fuck your head up and it’s been quite a nightmare at many points but things are looking up and I care more about taking care of myself than I ever have. It’s not easy and I still feel broken in many ways for many reasons but I’m thankful for my family and the small victories I make everyday. This is mostly a copy and paste from a post I made last year but I’m posting again for anyone it may encourage that change is possible. It’s not easy but it’s possible. TL;DR : Don’t do meth!
item.dc:creator: Grimishim

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Never Forget
item.description: On this day, 11 April 1945, as US forces approached, the inmate resistance seized control of Buchenwald concentration camp in Germany. However, when the Allies took control of the concentration camps many of those interned for homosexuality were not freed, but required to serve out the full term of their sentences under the homophobic Nazi penal code. Those who were released were not acknowledged as victims of Nazi persecution and were refused reparations. For Pride month, we have a number of LGBTQ history podcast episodes coming out, about the Stonewall riots and Lesbians and Gays Support the Miners. Subscribe today make sure you don't miss it: https://workingclasshistory.com/podcast Pictured: gay men in a concentration camp during the Holocaust #holocaust #lgbtq+ #onthisday #history
item.dc:creator: WholesomeAsFuck

item.title: Just a reminder that Marine Le Pen is a stupid cow
item.description: May the Streisand effect keep this image permanently circulating the internet. I'm sure getting a loan from a Russian bank is a perfectly normal and innocent thing for a French woman to do. /s
